items.title: A Consensus Statement on self-knowledge conceptualization, measurement, outcomes and changeability
items.author: Alicia Seidl
items.content: Self-knowledge plays a central role in contemporary psychological science. However, unresolved conceptual and methodological issues have hindered theoretical integration and cumulative scientific progress. In this Consensus Statement, Thielmann et al. identify gaps in four key areas of self-knowledge research: its conceptualization, measurement, outcomes and changeability.

items.title: Speech and language in healthy ageing and Alzheimer’s dementia
items.author: Adolfo M. García
items.content: Healthy ageing involves cognitive and functional declines, many of which intensify in Alzheimer’s dementia. In this Review, Garcia outlines key age-related changes in speech and language abilities and how these typical changes differ from changes observed in Alzheimer’s dementia.

items.title: Defining the concept of psychological trauma
items.author: Mirjam van Zuiden
items.content: A scientific consensus about what the term ‘trauma’ means has so far proven elusive. In this Review, Engelhard et al. revisit clinical psychology theories and findings to advance a common understanding of the emotional, contextual and cognitive aspects of psychological trauma.
